instruction: Planning the generation details for the following website:
input:
Design a dark-themed “PulseTrack Weekly Activity Dashboard” for a fitness app, optimized for desktop, showing a full-width header bar with the PulseTrack logo on the left, a subtle “Weekly Activity Overview” label, and a right-aligned date range picker for the selected week. Directly beneath the header, create a row of five prominent KPI tiles with large numeric values and smaller labels: “Total Steps This Week,” “Active Minutes,” “Workouts Completed,” “Calories Burned,” and “Distance Covered (km).” The central body is divided into three main columns: the left column’s top panel is a wide multi-line chart titled “Hourly Activity Intensity Today,” plotting smoothed curves for “Steps,” “Active Minutes,” and “Calories Burned” across the 24 hours, with small summary counters above the chart indicating today’s totals for each metric; directly under it, add a grouped bar chart titled “Workout Types by Day,” comparing counts of “Cardio,” “Strength,” “Yoga,” and “Other” for each weekday. The middle column’s top panel is a donut chart titled “Goal Completion Breakdown,” segmenting the proportion of completion for “Steps Goal,” “Workout Goal,” “Calories Goal,” “Distance Goal,” and “Active Minutes Goal,” with a legend on the right; beneath this, add a compact list-style panel titled “Goal Streak Highlights,” showing key goals with a label, the current streak in days, and a small value for “Best Streak” in a right-aligned column. The right column’s top panel is a vertical scrollable list of horizontal progress bars titled “Last Hour Activity by Device,” where each row shows a connected device name (for example “Phone Tracker,” “Smartwatch Alpha,” “Chest Strap Pro,” etc.), along with “Steps (last hour)” numeric values and visual progress toward a per-device hourly target; at the bottom of this column, include a panel titled “Friends Leaderboard (This Week)” listing friend display names with “Total Steps” and “Progress vs You (%)” so each row shows how they compare to the current user. All titles, labels, and numbers are in English, with clean sans-serif typography and high-contrast chart colors suitable for a modern analytics-style fitness dashboard.

instruction: Planning the generation details for the following website:
input:
Design a dark-themed “NovaFab Quality Command Center” dashboard for a multi-site manufacturing brand that monitors real-time product quality, defects, and downtime across plants. At the top left, place two slim horizontal bar gauges labeled “Overall Yield Rate” and “First Pass Yield,” each with animated tick marks and numeric values in percent, plus a subtle unit label “% of units” on the right. Beneath them, add two large circular dial gauges side by side, the left titled “Defect Rate – Current Shift” showing percentage with a colored needle sweeping across a 0–15% scale and a readout for “Min Today” and “Max Today” at the bottom, and the right titled “Downtime Ratio – Current Shift” with a 0–40% scale and two small values labeled “Planned” and “Unplanned” beneath. Directly under these dials, include two wide stacked bar and line combination charts; the upper one is “Hourly Defects by Line” showing bars per hour for three production lines and two overlaid moving-average defect rate lines, and the lower one is “Hourly Downtime by Cause” showing downtime minutes as bars per hour grouped by cause, again with a smoothed total downtime line. Centered on the page, add a stylized map of a single country or region in monochrome, titled “Quality by Plant Location,” with each plant site highlighted; hovering a plant shows its current yield, defect rate, and downtime minutes, and one plant is emphasized with a brighter color to indicate the selected site. On the right side top, include a compact information panel labeled “Selected Plant: Aurora Works” that shows the current date, shift code, plant ID, and a bold numeric “Current OEE” (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) in large font with supporting metrics such as “Throughput (units/h), Scrap (units),” and “Changeovers Today” listed below. Just under that, add a radial spider chart titled “Defect Profile Radar” with axes for “Surface Flaws,” “Dimensional Errors,” “Assembly Issues,” “Packaging,” “Labeling,” and “Other,” shading the polygon for the selected plant. To the right of the radar chart, create two vertically stacked circular mini-gauges, the first labeled “Current Line Speed” with a numeric value in units per minute and the second labeled “Equipment Health Index” with a 0–100 score and color-coded band. At the lower right, mirror the grid of summary tiles with four rectangular stat cards under the heading “Downtime Summary,” arranged in a 2x2 layout; the top row contains “Last 24 Hours” and “Last 30 Days” cards, each displaying “Total Downtime (min)” and “Top Cause,” while the bottom row shows “Weekly Average” and “Year-to-Date” with “Avg Downtime per Day (min)” and “Availability %.” Across the very bottom spanning most of the width, place a long, thin timeline slider labeled “Shift Timeline Navigator” with tick marks for hours and a draggable handle that controls which time window is highlighted in the charts above.

instruction: Planning the generation details for the following website:
input:
Design a full-screen “NovaBoost Campaign Performance Center” dashboard with a dark, neon-accented theme, keeping a three-column layout and a strong central focus on real-time KPIs. At the very top, show a slim header bar with the product logo “NovaBoost” on the left, the title “Global Campaign Performance Dashboard” in the center, and the current date and time on the right. Directly beneath the title in the center, place a large horizontal counter showing “Current Active Impressions” as five glowing flip-style number tiles that update in real time. Under that counter, in the center of the page, feature a 3D-style illustration card of a marketing control hub, subtly animated to suggest data streaming in from multiple channels. On the left column, the top panel is a donut chart titled “Audience Gender Split” showing the share of male, female, and unspecified impressions for the current month. Beneath it, place a dual-ring radial chart titled “Ad Inventory Utilization” showing “Available Slots” vs “Booked Slots” as two concentric arcs. Below that, include a horizontal stacked bar chart titled “Campaign Funnel Stages” displaying the count of users across stages “Awareness,” “Consideration,” “Trial,” “Purchase,” and “Loyalty,” with each bar segmented by “Email,” “Social,” and “Search.” At the bottom left, add a small line-and-bar combo chart titled “Landing Page Conversion” showing “Visits” and “Conversions” over the last 7 days. In the right column, mirror the vertical stacking: at the top, use a smooth dual-line chart titled “Daily Reach vs Clicks by Channel” with the x-axis labeled by days and two lines for “Reach” and “Clicks.” Directly under it, place a horizontal stacked bar chart titled “Budget Allocation by Region” comparing four regions with segments for “Planned Spend” and “Actual Spend.” At the bottom right, add a dense data table titled “Campaign Performance Breakdown” listing campaign names with columns for impressions, clicks, conversions, and cost per acquisition. Finally, along the bottom center of the page, include a semi-circular gauge labeled “Overall ROI Score” showing the current percentage of return for all NovaBoost campaigns combined, with tick marks from 0 to 100 and a needle indicating the live value.
